item.title: Alone at the office today
item.description: We’ve started to return to our offices, but I was the only of my colleagues in today and one of like five people in the building, so I did the only reasonable thing: reenact the Pablo Escobar waiting meme.

item.title: Welp. I thought i could salvage it
item.description: It's okay if this dies in usersub, i think i just need to vent. My parents found my testosterone prescription and so I ended up telling them I'm bi and nonbinary. They've been pretty horrible about it, calling me disgusting and saying I should be ashamed of myself. Specifically they think I'm a pervert for being bi. I tried to come out to my mom when I was fifteen and ended up taking it back because I was scared. Just said it was a phase. Now that I'm 24 and they've forced me to come out more or less, this is what's happened. Weirdly enough, I wanna live. For the first time, I'm more comfortable with myself and my life than I ever have been before. And I want my parents in my life, I do. But apparently they don't want me, so. I guess that's that. The past month or so has been pretty hellish because of this. I've separated my finances from my parents and am planning to move out in a few months. I thought I could make this work but I can't. I just wanna be comfortable in my own skin. But I wanna live. And I guess I can't do that with them until I can get some space, at the least. Thanks for reading. Please tell the queer people in your life that you love them. I have people that love and support me, but it's been a bit since I've seen em. Remind your loved ones. Edit: thank you all so much for your love and support. I know I deserve love and happiness, sometimes it's just hard to see the light. I'm grateful for the reminder that i can get through this. I'm gonna look back on these comments on hard days, thank you so much ❤️❤️❤️
item.dc:creator: ahmackalak

item.dc:creator: GoodBadInternet

item.title: You crazy MFers DID THIS shit
item.description: On August 2, 2015, Imgurians changed my life. I posted a story about how I'd lost my job, almost lost my home, and that depression was taking over... ...but through many sleepless nights (and tubs of ice cream) I built my writing career to a point where it could actually pay my bills. Knowing what it's like to be broke as hell, I shared my story to the Imgur community, and gave away a Kindle version of Arena Mode for free. THEN....SHIT EXPLODED. Arena Mode became a trilogy, then it was optioned for a streaming series, and NOW I have a sexy new cover, and Heavy Metal Magazine is re-releasing the novel in bookstores worldwide. Being able to afford fancy luxuries like food and heat are nice, don't get me wrong. But above all else, I made lifelong friends. I received so much support from Imgurians that it still makes me tear up to think about it (support I was NOT getting from my family back in 2015) I can't thank you enough, because I would NOT BE HERE if it wasn't for you. Love and hugs from Canada xox - Blake -- MOST VIRAL EDIT: Hail Satan! Kidding. At the risk of sounding like an Oscar acceptance speech, THANK YOU again. Love you guys. If you want to know more about Arena Mode: http://www.arenamode.com/ And YES, there will be a shout-out to Imgur when the streaming series drops. That's my only special request for the credits. ;)
item.dc:creator: BlakeNorthcott

item.title: Russia is attacking the largest nuclear power plant in Europe
item.description: Thread following the action: https://twitter.com/NotWoofers/status/1499527941377732609?t=gbOaBE0dFrPDMBErf-OxIg&s=19 Live stream from the plant: https://youtu.be/fYUT36YGOh8 Fuck Putin Update: Seems like things are settling down. There's a fire, but it's at a separate museum rather than a reactor or cooling tower. The IAEA is reporting that there hasn't been any change in radiation levels. So far, seems like we've avoided any sort of disaster here, but attacking nuclear power plants is generally against the Geneva Convention because of the risk involved (also not supposed to attack dams or dykes because of risk to civilians). So, Fuck Putin.
item.dc:creator: jderig

item.dc:creator: RoseBeef

item.title: Real Men need what?
item.description: Art: J Flores https://twitter.com/J_Flores_Draws Thank you so much to @AmishBarn420RaiseIt for a link leading to the artist. Text copied from or inspired by: https://egalitarianchica.tumblr.com/ Normalize taking care of your man the same way you would want him to take care of you. Not like a little boy - he's not. Society (private and personal) have not prepared most men or women for looking at men this way or this kind of communication. Normalize holding and kissing them when they’re sad and just because. Normalize doing nice things for them. Normalize genuinely listening to them when they’re upset. Normalize complimenting them and telling them you love and appreciate them. It’s great that we idolize male celebrities (and men in general) for how well they love their women; we need to show women and girls what they’re worthy of. Men see this in society - sometimes it's oversold or soppy. But it's there. But can we also start doing the reverse, idolizing female celebrities (and women in general) for how well they love their men? Men and boys need to be shown what they’re worthy of too. This is not a momentary or a fad thing. I'm not talking about holidays and birthdays. It's a lifestyle change and takes love and commitment moments when you don't always feel like it - men do the same. Finally, sex was an easy target but I also don’t mean housewife stuff like cooking and cleaning. I mean that women should be affectionate with their male partners and provide them with emotional support and do sincere romantic gestures for them sometimes, not just Hallmark cards (nice but not the point here). What is your first thought after reading this?
item.dc:creator: epicurusaquinas

item.title: I know I am late, but I just switched from Internet Explorer. My 13th favorite.
item.description: Shiny. Front page edit. First Front Page ever. After my previous record being 27 upvotes, this feels pretty good. @tjlasershepard thank you for posting this so I could favorite it. Thank you imgurians for the upvotes, and a huge Jewel for being Jewel. Send me pictures of hot, sexy, bacon.

item.title: Remember when RT news anchor Liz Wahl resigned live on air over Ukraine crisis?
item.description: Liz Wahl of the Washington bureau of the Kremlin-funded Russia Today TV station stepped down in March 2014 during a live broadcast after criticizing the station's coverage of Russia's decision to send troops into Crimea. "Personally, I cannot be part of a network funded by the Russian government, that whitewashes the actions of (Russian President Vladimir) Putin," said Wahl. "I'm proud to be an American and believe in disseminating the truth and that is why, after this newscast, I'm resigning." Today it was announced that RT America, the sister network to Russia’s state-sponsored RT, is ceasing production after being dropped by DirecTV and Roku following the invasion of Ukraine. T&R Productions, the production entity, sent a note to employees announcing the shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Yaratam

item.title: Message from the defender of Irpin, Ukraine
item.description: “Dear Ukrainians, Irpinians. I want to tell you that Irpin and Bucha are courageously defending the borders. No one betrayed anyone or anything. We stand our ground. Armed Forces of Ukraine, Security Forces stand as one, shoulder to shoulder. Don’t listen to random fakes. We defend our country. All is well. God is with us. Ukraine is with us. We will never give up Irpin by an inch. We will fight to the death.”
